content: Always use keyword arguments for functions with more than two parameters. Use dataclasses or Pydantic models for structured data, never raw dicts for domain objects. Use pathlib.Path over os.path. Use 'from __future__ import annotations' for modern type syntax.

Routes must not contain business logic — delegate to services. Services must not import route modules or Pydantic request schemas. Pydantic schemas must not contain behavior beyond validators. ORM models must not contain business logic. Higher layers depend on lower layers only — no circular imports.

name: Backend dependency flow
description: api/routes/ → services/ → adapters/, models/, workers/. api/websocket/ → services/. services/ → adapters/, models/, core/. adapters/ implement ModelAdapter interface per model family. models/ are SQLAlchemy ORM only. schemas/ are Pydantic request/response shapes. core/ holds database, config, events.

content: Never use 'Any' — use 'object' or type narrowing. All function parameters and return types must have type annotations. Use TypedDict for dictionary shapes crossing API boundaries. Use Literal types instead of magic strings. Use Protocol for structural typing. Pydantic models define all API request/response shapes.

SQLite is the only database. All schema changes go through Alembic migrations. Database sessions must use async context managers. Never store large binary data in SQLite — store filesystem paths instead. Metric points should be batch-inserted for performance during training.

name: fastapi-async-setup
description: FastAPI async setup: use asynccontextmanager lifespan for startup (create dirs, init db), async_sessionmaker with expire_on_commit=False for sessions, aiosqlite driver in DATABASE_URL (sqlite+aiosqlite://). All models use Mapped[] typed columns with from __future__ import annotations.

name: sqlalchemy-orm-circular-imports
description: Avoid circular imports in SQLAlchemy ORM models: use TYPE_CHECKING guard for relationship type hints, and string-based relationship() references ('ClassName') so SQLAlchemy resolves at mapper configuration time, not import time.

name: alembic-circular-fk
description: For tables with circular FKs (projects <-> config_versions), write Alembic migration manually with use_alter=True on the deferred FK constraint. Create config_versions first (without the FK that references projects), then projects (with the deferred FK to config_versions), then add the constraint.

content: For flat-layout pyproject.toml (backend/ at root, not src/), must configure [tool.setuptools.packages.find] with where=['.'] and include=['app*'] to avoid 'Multiple top-level packages' error from setuptools autodiscovery.

name: workbench-config-schema
description: WorkbenchConfig Pydantic hierarchy (13 sub-models + top-level) defined in backend/app/schemas/workbench_config.py. Use Literal[] for all enum-like string fields, union types for optional fields (str | None), and from __future__ import annotations at top. All 14 classes match spec.md section 12.3 exactly.

name: fastapi-annotated-depends
description: FastAPI Depends() must use Annotated type alias to satisfy ruff B008: DbSession = Annotated[AsyncSession, Depends(get_db_session)], then use as plain parameter type annotation in route handlers.
SQLAlchemy 2.x does not export 'Real' at top level. Existing models (run.py, metric_point.py, suggestion.py) had 'from sqlalchemy import Real' causing ImportError.
resolution: Replace Real with Float in all SQLAlchemy model imports.

name: settings-override-pattern
description: Global app settings (SettingsUpdate/GET/PATCH) implemented as in-memory _overrides dict merged with pydantic-settings AppConfig defaults. Persisted to data/settings.json on PATCH. Loaded on startup via _load_persisted_overrides() called in lifespan.

name: config-diff-format
description: Config version diffs stored as JSON in diff_from_prev column. Computed by flattening both YAML dicts with dot-notation keys then comparing. Format: {changed: {key: {old, new}}, added: {key: val}, removed: {key: val}}. ConfigVersionResponse uses field_validator to parse JSON string to dict on read.

name: model-resolve-meta-device
description: Use torch.device('meta') + AutoModelForCausalLM.from_config() to count parameters without loading weights. Fast, no memory allocation. Falls back to zero counts if meta instantiation fails.

name: in-memory-model-dataset-state
description: Model profiles, adapters, activation snapshots, and dataset samples stored in module-level dicts keyed by project_id. Same pattern as settings-override-pattern. Viable for single-user local app.

name: weight-delta-computation
description: compute_weight_deltas in introspection.py loads state dicts from safetensors (glob *.safetensors) or .bin shards, computes L2 norm of (after - before) per parameter key, returns WeightDeltaResponse with layers sorted by l2_norm desc and total_l2_norm as root-sum-square. Route is POST /projects/{id}/models/deltas. CheckpointNotFoundError raised for missing paths.

name: main-py-phase4-integration
description: main.py integration pattern: import runs router (app.api.routes.runs), websocket router (app.api.websocket.handler), and watchdog recovery (app.services.watchdog.recover_stale_runs); include all three in lifespan after create_tables and _load_persisted_overrides
ConfigVersion ORM model uses yaml_blob not yaml_content for the YAML content column.
resolution: Use config_version.yaml_blob in orchestrator._resolve_config_path when writing config to disk.

name: websocket-connection-manager
description: ConnectionManager in stream.py holds per-connection state (_ClientConnection dataclass), bounded asyncio.Queue (256 items) for backpressure, one sender drain task per connection, event_handlers dict keyed by id(websocket) for cleanup. handler.py is thin: accept, connect, message loop, disconnect. resource_update events emitted every 5s via psutil+torch (cuda/mps fallback to 0.0).

name: ai-recommender-pattern
description: RecommendationEngine ABC in services/ai_recommender.py with RuleBasedEngine (7-rule fallback) and CloudLLMEngine (Anthropic SDK + OpenAI). build_engine() selects engine based on settings. suggestion_service.py handles generate (runs engine, stores), accept (applies config diff to YAML, creates config version with source_tag=ai_suggestion, logs decision), reject. Suggestion config_diff stored as JSON string in DB.

name: storage-management-pattern
description: Phase 6 storage system: StorageRecord ORM (category, total_bytes, file_count) with ProjectStorageResponse schema (breakdown, per_run summaries, retention_policy). Policy: keep_last_n (3), always_keep_best_eval, always_keep_final, delete_intermediates_after_completion. Mark artifacts with is_retained=0. storage_manager service: compute storage, apply policy, log to decision_logs. Download via Content-Disposition header.
